title: teucrium marum subsp. marum
intro: Teucrium marum subsp. marum, also known as the Common Foxglove or Wound Wort, is a flowering plant species within the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides and other compounds. The health benefits associated with Teucrium marum subsp. marum include wound healing, antimicrobial activity, and potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
